result:
The fix is complete. Here's a summary:

## Root Cause

In `TrailingCommaOnDeclarationSiteRule.kt`, the `visitClass` method processes enum classes to check for trailing commas after enum entries. When an empty enum class (e.g., `enum class Foo {}`) is encountered, the method calls `findNodeAfterTrailingCommaPosition()` which executes:

```kotlin
val lastEnumEntry = children().last { it.psi is KtEnumEntry }
```

Since there are no `KtEnumEntry` children in an empty enum, `.last { ... }` throws `NoSuchElementException: Sequence contains no element matching the predicate`.

## Fix

Added a `takeUnless` guard in the `visitClass` method at `TrailingCommaOnDeclarationSiteRule.kt:200-203` that skips processing when the enum class body has no entries:

```kotlin
?.takeUnless {
    // Do nothing when enum class has no entries
    it.children().none { child -> child.psi is KtEnumEntry }
}
```

This is placed before the existing `lastTwoEnumEntriesAreOnSameLine()` check, so the chain short-circuits early when the enum is empty, preventing the crash in `findNodeAfterTrailingCommaPosition()`.

The change is minimal (3 lines added) and only affects the single file where the bug originates. All existing tests continue to pass.
